mnemonics: ARGC-V Counts Values: ARGument Count (argc) tells you how many Values are in ARGV
keyPoints: The main function signature is int main(int argc, char *argv[])
argv[0] is the program name, argv[1] is the first actual argument
argv[argc] is always a NULL pointer
commonMistakes: Starting argument processing from argv[0] (the program name) instead of argv[1]
Forgetting that argv elements are strings, not numbers (requires conversion with atoi() or strtol())
Accessing argv[argc] which is out of bounds (should stop at argc-1)
